title: Inflation data, big bank earnings, and Netflix results: What to watch this week A busy week ahead for investors will see inflation data, the debate about the Fed's next move, and the start of second quarter earnings season all come into focus after a flurry of trade activity last week.

Bitcoin soars to new all-time high as US lawmakers focus on pro-crypto legislation Bitcoin continued its rapid climb and hit another all-time high Monday as U.S. lawmakers begin a week focused on passing pro-crypto legislation.  The world’s oldest and most popular cryptocurrency is currently the fifth most valuable asset class in the world at $2.4 trillion, giving it a higher market cap than Amazon.  The enthusiasm for bitcoin comes as the U.S. House is set to take up several pieces of cryptocurrency-related legislation in what’s been dubbed “crypto week” in Congress.

GreenPal Empowers Lawn Care Pros Leveraging AI, Surpasses 5 Million Transactions GreenPal, the leading software platform built specifically for lawn care businesses, today celebrates surpassing 5 million completed lawn care transactions nationwide. The achievement highlights GreenPal's role in empowering lawn care professionals to efficiently manage and scale their businesses through innovative AI-powered tools to grow their small businesses.

(NASDAQ: PEGA), the Enterprise Transformation Company™, today announced it has signed a five-year strategic collaboration agreement (SCA) with Amazon Web Services (AWS). The collaboration will empower clients to harness the power of Pega products with AWS advanced generative AI services. Bug Bite Thing Now Offering Tick Testing Bug Bite Thing, the leader in providing consumers with chemical-free first aid products, including its insect bite relief suction tool and its tick remover tweezer tool, is expanding its offerings to include tick testing. The new service is powered by TickReport, a trusted industry leader, which has provided consumers with accurate tick testing results for over eighteen years and has tested over 300,000 ticks.
